title: Availability, Disruption Budgets, Graceful Draining, Autoscaling, and Capacity · API and Operations Recall
summary: An on-call handoff reconstructs availability, disruption budgets, graceful draining, autoscaling, and capacity from delayed object status, challenges one retained misconception, and revises the availability and autoscaling operating policy under changed capacity. Planned artifact: Extend the evidence record for availability and autoscaling operating policy after a delayed changed case. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence d…
objectives: Translate service objectives into replica count, failure domains, topology placement, readiness, surge capacity, dependency limits, and recovery evidence.
Calculate minAvailable or maxUnavailable, unhealthy eviction policy, controller scale, simultaneous drains, and voluntary versus involuntary disruption limits.
Derive HPA desired replicas from requests, utilization, custom or external metrics, missing samples, tolerance, stabilization, and scale policies.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# kubernetes-basics cumulative orchestration portfolio
# Browser labs perform deterministic manifest and evidence review only.
# They never invoke kubectl, read kubeconfig, contact a cluster API, or execute host commands.
workspace: kubernetes
version-baseline:
  kubernetes: 1.36.2
  minikube: 1.38.1
  gateway-api: 1.5.1
  helm: 4.2.2
transfer-boundary: authorized-disposable-cluster
evidence:

steps.title: K8s Replica Failure Domain Capacity · changed case · Review K8s Replica Failure Domain Capacity
steps.instruction:
review case — A review service has 5 replicas but all share one zone, a strict disruption budget blocks drain, and HPA oscillates because requests and metrics are wrong. Balance failure domains, PDB, rollout surge, autoscaling signals, stabilization, and capacity. Competency probe: Translate service objectives into replica count, failure domains, topology placement, readiness, surge capacity, dependency limits, and recovery evidence.
